strengths: 18+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map globally
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows
concerns: No director-level or principal-level scope: career history is senior/staff IC roles focused on delivery, with no evidence of organization-wide technical strategy or executive-level influence
No recognized AI expertise: AI exposure is limited to ~1 year of tooling work; no shipped AI products or platforms, publications, or advanced degree in an AI-related field
Cloud experience is applied (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than strategic; no evidence of advising strategic customers or defining future-state cloud product direction

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI-built products
15+ years engineering experience with entrepreneurial background

response.justification: The role's core competencies are (1) 15+ years of engineering experience — met, with roughly 18 years spanning National Instruments C/C++ work to current full-stack roles; (2) recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products — largely absent, as the only cited evidence is recent MCP/agentic tooling contributions at re:cinq with no shipped AI product or platform of record; (3) cloud fluency at a strategic level — partially present via AWS CDK and Kubernetes-based deployment of GISCollective, but applied rather than advisory; (4) entrepreneurial experience — met through founding GISCollective; and (5) director-level influence and inspiring others externally — weak, evidenced only by conference talks (FOSS4G 2019) and regional workshops rather than industry-level thought leadership or strategic customer advisory. Strong senior engineer, but the gap on the two highest-weight competencies (AI recognition and principal/director scope) drives the score down.
